question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exhaust all available database TCP connections from the network perimeter, causing DatabaseWaiter to loop through the full `timeout` window (default 5 minutes) with `interval`-spaced retries, blocking all block ingestion and transaction processing for 500%+ of average block time?
